---
title: "SCROL Matrix Report: `r params$company`"
subtitle: "Supply Chain Resilience Opportunities & Limitations"
author:
  - name: Supply Chain Finance Lectoraat
    affiliations:
      - name: Hogeschool Windesheim
        address: Campus 2, Zwolle
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    classoption: ["oneside", "fontsize=11pt"]
    link-citations: false
    number-sections: false
    toc: false
    titlepage: "bg-image"
    titlepage-bg-image: "img/corner-bg.png"
    titlepage-logo: "img/logo.png"
    titlepage-header: "Resilience Scan | NEXT GEN Logistics Initiative"
    titlepage-footer: |
      Supply Chain Finance Lectoraat, Hogeschool Windesheim\
      https://resiliencescan.org/ | https://www.windesheim.com/research/professorships/supply-chain-finance
    coverpage-include-file:
      - tex/copyright.tex
    titlepage-include-file:
      - tex/dedication.tex
    titlepage-theme:
      vrule-color: "004D40"
      vrule-width: "10pt"
    coverpage: otter
    coverpage-bg-image: "img/otter-bar.jpeg"
    coverpage-title: "resiliencescan"
    coverpage-author: ["Supply Chain Finance Lectoraat, Hogeschool Windesheim", "NEXT GEN Logistics"]
    coverpage-theme:
      title-color: "white"
      title-fontfamily: "QTDublinIrish.otf"
      title-fontsize: 70
      author-style: "plain"
      author-sep: "newline"
      author-fontstyle: ["textbf", "textsc"]
      author-fontsize: 24
      author-color: "4CAF50"
      author-align: "right"
      author-bottom: "1.5in"
      footer-style: "none"
      header-style: "none"
      date-style: "none"
    keep-tex: false
    header-includes: |
      \usepackage{geometry}
      \usepackage{graphicx}
      \usepackage{float}
      \usepackage{array}
      \usepackage{booktabs}
      \usepackage{longtable}
      \usepackage{multirow}
      \usepackage{colortbl}
      \usepackage{xcolor}
      \usepackage{pdflscape}
      \usepackage{tabu}
      \usepackage{threeparttable}
      \usepackage{threeparttablex}
      \usepackage[normalem]{ulem}
      \usepackage{makecell}
      \usepackage{caption}
      \usepackage{fancyhdr}
      \usepackage{setspace}
      \usepackage{hyperref}
      \usepackage{microtype}
      \geometry{margin=2.5cm}
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
  freeze: false
params:
  company: "Placeholder Company"
  person: ""
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(fmsb)
library(scales)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)

options(scipen = 999, digits = 2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.pos = "H", out.extra = "")
```

```{r load-data, include=FALSE}
# ── Data loading ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
data_file_path <- "data/cleaned_master.csv"

score_cols <- c(
  "up__r", "up__c", "up__f", "up__v", "up__a",
  "in__r", "in__c", "in__f", "in__v", "in__a",
  "do__r", "do__c", "do__f", "do__v", "do__a"
)

respondent <- NULL
data_source <- "synthetic"

if (file.exists(data_file_path)) {
  tryCatch({
    df <- readr::read_csv(data_file_path,
      col_types = readr::cols(.default = "c"),
      show_col_types = FALSE
    )
    df <- df %>% rename_with(~ str_to_lower(str_trim(.)))

    # Match company
    co_col <- intersect(c("company_name", "company"), colnames(df))[1]
    if (!is.na(co_col)) {
      matches <- which(str_to_lower(str_trim(df[[co_col]])) ==
                       str_to_lower(str_trim(params$company)))
      if (length(matches) > 0) {
        # Prefer person match if provided
        person_target <- if (!is.null(params$person)) str_trim(params$person) else ""
        if (person_target != "" && "name" %in% colnames(df)) {
          pm <- which(str_to_lower(str_trim(df$name[matches])) ==
                      str_to_lower(person_target))
          if (length(pm) > 0) matches <- matches[pm[1]]
        }
        respondent <- df[matches[1], , drop = FALSE]
        data_source <- "real"
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}

# ── Convert score columns to numeric ────────────────────────────────────────
as_score <- function(x) {
  v <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", gsub("[^0-9.,-]", "", as.character(x)))))
  ifelse(is.na(v), NA_real_, pmax(0, pmin(5, v)))
}

if (!is.null(respondent)) {
  for (col in score_cols) {
    if (col %in% colnames(respondent)) {
      respondent[[col]] <- as_score(respondent[[col]])
    } else {
      respondent[[col]] <- NA_real_
    }
  }
} else {
  # Synthetic fallback — reproducible seed from company name
  set.seed(abs(sum(utf8ToInt(params$company))) %% .Machine$integer.max)
  scores <- pmax(0.5, pmin(4.8, rnorm(15, mean = 3.0, sd = 0.7)))
  respondent <- as.data.frame(setNames(as.list(scores), score_cols))
  respondent$company_name <- params$company
  data_source <- "synthetic"
}

# ── Pillar scores ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_score <- function(col) {
  v <- respondent[[col]]
  if (length(v) == 0 || is.na(v)) NA_real_ else as.numeric(v[1])
}

up_r <- get_score("up__r"); up_c <- get_score("up__c")
up_f <- get_score("up__f"); up_v <- get_score("up__v"); up_a <- get_score("up__a")

in_r <- get_score("in__r"); in_c <- get_score("in__c")
in_f <- get_score("in__f"); in_v <- get_score("in__v"); in_a <- get_score("in__a")

do_r <- get_score("do__r"); do_c <- get_score("do__c")
do_f <- get_score("do__f"); do_v <- get_score("do__v"); do_a <- get_score("do__a")

# Column averages across all three supply chain layers
avg_r  <- mean(c(up_r, in_r, do_r), na.rm = TRUE)
avg_c  <- mean(c(up_c, in_c, do_c), na.rm = TRUE)
avg_f  <- mean(c(up_f, in_f, do_f), na.rm = TRUE)
avg_v  <- mean(c(up_v, in_v, do_v), na.rm = TRUE)
avg_a  <- mean(c(up_a, in_a, do_a), na.rm = TRUE)

# Row averages
up_avg  <- mean(c(up_r, up_c, up_f, up_v, up_a),   na.rm = TRUE)
in_avg  <- mean(c(in_r, in_c, in_f, in_v, in_a),   na.rm = TRUE)
do_avg  <- mean(c(do_r, do_c, do_f, do_v, do_a),   na.rm = TRUE)
all_avg <- mean(c(up_avg, in_avg, do_avg),           na.rm = TRUE)

# ── Colour coding (0-5 scale) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
score_color <- function(s) {
  if (is.na(s) || !is.finite(s)) return("D3D3D3")
  if (s < 2) return("E74C3C")
  if (s < 3) return("E67E22")
  if (s < 4) return("F4D03F")
  return("27AE60")
}

text_color_is_white <- function(s) {
  !is.na(s) && is.finite(s) && s < 3
}

fmt <- function(s) if (is.na(s) || !is.finite(s)) "N/A" else sprintf("%.2f", s)

# Build a LaTeX cell with background colour via \cellcolor[HTML]{}
colored_cell <- function(s) {
  bg  <- score_color(s)
  val <- fmt(s)
  if (text_color_is_white(s)) {
    paste0("\\cellcolor[HTML]{", bg, "}\\textcolor{white}{", val, "}")
  } else {
    paste0("\\cellcolor[HTML]{", bg, "}", val)
  }
}
```

# SCROL Matrix — `r params$company`

**Respondent:** `r if (!is.null(params$person) && nzchar(params$person)) params$person else "—"`
**Data source:** `r if (data_source == "real") "Survey data" else "Synthetic (company not found in dataset)"`

The **Supply Chain Resilience Opportunities & Limitations (SCROL)** matrix maps resilience
capability scores across five dimensions and three supply chain layers. Scores range from
**0** (absent) to **5** (excellent).

```{r colour-legend, results='asis'}
legend_df <- data.frame(
  Level     = c("< 2.0", "2.0 – 2.99", "3.0 – 3.99", "≥ 4.0"),
  Meaning   = c("Critical — immediate attention required",
                "Below average — improvement needed",
                "Average — further development recommended",
                "Strong — maintain and leverage")
)
legend_df %>%
  kable(booktabs = TRUE, col.names = c("Score range", "Interpretation"),
        caption = "SCROL colour legend") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, background = "#E74C3C", color = "white") %>%
  row_spec(2, background = "#E67E22", color = "white") %>%
  row_spec(3, background = "#F4D03F") %>%
  row_spec(4, background = "#27AE60")
```

\vspace{0.5em}

```{r scrol-matrix, results='asis'}
# Write the matrix as raw LaTeX — kable mangles \cellcolor even with escape=FALSE
lc <- function(s, bold = FALSE) {
  bg  <- score_color(s)
  val <- fmt(s)
  inner <- if (bold) paste0("\\textbf{", val, "}") else val
  if (text_color_is_white(s)) {
    paste0("\\cellcolor[HTML]{", bg, "}\\textcolor{white}{", inner, "}")
  } else {
    paste0("\\cellcolor[HTML]{", bg, "}{", inner, "}")
  }
}
hc <- function(txt) paste0("\\textcolor{white}{\\textbf{", txt, "}}")

co <- gsub("[&%$#_{}^~\\\\]", " ", params$company)  # sanitise for LaTeX caption

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\small\n")
cat(paste0("\\caption{SCROL Matrix -- ", co, "}\n"))
cat("\\begin{tabular}{lcccccc}\n")
cat("\\hline\n")
cat(paste0(
  "\\rowcolor[HTML]{2C3E50} ",
  paste(sapply(c("Layer","Redundancy","Collaboration","Flexibility","Visibility","Agility","Row Avg"), hc),
        collapse = " & "),
  " \\\\\n"
))
cat("\\hline\n")

rows_data <- list(
  list("\\textbf{Overall}",  avg_r, avg_c, avg_f, avg_v, avg_a, all_avg),
  list("Upstream",           up_r,  up_c,  up_f,  up_v,  up_a,  up_avg),
  list("Internal",           in_r,  in_c,  in_f,  in_v,  in_a,  in_avg),
  list("Downstream",         do_r,  do_c,  do_f,  do_v,  do_a,  do_avg)
)

for (r in rows_data) {
  cells <- paste(sapply(r[-1], lc), collapse = " & ")
  cat(paste0(r[[1]], " & ", cells, " \\\\\n"))
}

cat("\\hline\n")
cat("\\end{tabular}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n")
```

\vspace{1em}

# Radar Charts by Supply Chain Layer

```{r radar-charts, fig.width=7, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Resilience radar charts per supply chain layer (scale 0–5)"}
# fmsb radar: first two rows are max and min
# Short labels for radar charts (fmsb clips long strings)
dims        <- c("Redundancy", "Collaboration", "Flexibility", "Visibility", "Agility")
dims_short  <- c("Redundancy", "Collab.", "Flexibility", "Visibility", "Agility")

make_radar <- function(scores, title, fill_col) {
  df_radar <- rbind(
    rep(5, 5),   # max
    rep(0, 5),   # min
    scores
  )
  df_radar <- as.data.frame(df_radar)
  colnames(df_radar) <- dims_short

  op <- par(mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
  fmsb::radarchart(
    df_radar,
    axistype  = 1,
    pcol      = fill_col,
    pfcol     = scales::alpha(fill_col, 0.25),
    plwd      = 2,
    cglcol    = "grey70",
    cglty     = 1,
    axislabcol = "grey50",
    caxislabels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5"),
    vlcex     = 0.85,
    title     = title
  )
  par(op)
}

layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

make_radar(c(avg_r, avg_c, avg_f, avg_v, avg_a), "Supply Chain Overall", "#2C3E50")
make_radar(c(up_r,  up_c,  up_f,  up_v,  up_a),  "Upstream",             "#2980B9")
make_radar(c(in_r,  in_c,  in_f,  in_v,  in_a),  "Internal",             "#8E44AD")
make_radar(c(do_r,  do_c,  do_f,  do_v,  do_a),  "Downstream",           "#27AE60")
```

\newpage

# Score Summary

```{r bar-chart, fig.width=6.5, fig.height=4, fig.cap="Resilience scores by supply chain layer and dimension"}
bar_data <- data.frame(
  Layer = rep(c("Upstream", "Internal", "Downstream"), each = 5),
  Dimension = rep(dims, 3),
  Score = c(up_r, up_c, up_f, up_v, up_a,
            in_r, in_c, in_f, in_v, in_a,
            do_r, do_c, do_f, do_v, do_a)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Layer = factor(Layer, levels = c("Upstream", "Internal", "Downstream")),
    Dimension = factor(Dimension, levels = dims),
    Band = case_when(
      is.na(Score) ~ "N/A",
      Score < 2    ~ "Critical (< 2)",
      Score < 3    ~ "Below average (2–3)",
      Score < 4    ~ "Average (3–4)",
      TRUE         ~ "Strong (≥ 4)"
    ),
    Band = factor(Band, levels = c("Critical (< 2)", "Below average (2–3)",
                                   "Average (3–4)", "Strong (≥ 4)", "N/A"))
  )

band_colors <- c(
  "Critical (< 2)"       = "#E74C3C",
  "Below average (2–3)"  = "#E67E22",
  "Average (3–4)"        = "#F4D03F",
  "Strong (≥ 4)"         = "#27AE60",
  "N/A"                  = "#D3D3D3"
)

ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = Dimension, y = Score, fill = Band)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(2, 3, 4), linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = band_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5), breaks = 0:5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Layer, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Score (0–5)", fill = "Performance band") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r summary-table, results='asis'}
# Row averages with interpretation
interp <- function(s) {
  if (is.na(s) || !is.finite(s)) return("N/A")
  if (s < 2) return("Critical")
  if (s < 3) return("Below average")
  if (s < 4) return("Average")
  return("Strong")
}

summary_df <- data.frame(
  Layer          = c("Supply Chain Overall", "Upstream", "Internal", "Downstream"),
  `Average Score` = c(fmt(all_avg), fmt(up_avg), fmt(in_avg), fmt(do_avg)),
  Interpretation = c(interp(all_avg), interp(up_avg), interp(in_avg), interp(do_avg)),
  check.names    = FALSE
)

summary_df %>%
  kable(booktabs = TRUE, align = c("l", "c", "l"),
        caption = "Layer averages and interpretation") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#2C3E50", color = "white") %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

# Interpretation Guide

| Capability | Description |
|---|---|
| **Redundancy** | Backup capacity, buffer stock, and alternative sourcing |
| **Collaboration** | Information sharing and joint planning with supply chain partners |
| **Flexibility** | Ability to adapt volumes, products, and suppliers rapidly |
| **Visibility** | Real-time insight into supply chain status and risks |
| **Agility** | Speed of response to disruptions and market changes |

: SCROL capability definitions

\vspace{0.5em}

*This report was generated by the ResilienceScan platform, Supply Chain Finance Lectoraat,
Hogeschool Windesheim. Scores are derived from validated survey responses and represent
the respondent's self-assessed supply chain resilience profile.*
